summary: IDF forces engaged Hezbollah militants in south Lebanon, killing multiple fighters and conducting airstrikes on Beit Yahoun. Iran-linked forces attacked a Singapore‑flagged vessel in the Strait of Hormuz and shelled a civilian ship in the Gulf of Oman. Violent clan clashes and explosions were reported in Syria and Iraq, raising regional tension.
key_points: IDF killed five Hezbollah militants in Zoter A‑Sharkiya and a gunman on Mount Ali Tahr in south Lebanon (Source: @idf_telegram/24445).
Israeli airstrikes hit the town of Beit Yahoun in southern Lebanon amid ceasefire violations (Source: @presstv/195650).
Iran’s Revolutionary Guard attacked a Singapore‑flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @naya_foriraq/79887).
Revolutionary Guard forces fired a shell at a civilian cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman without passage permits (Source: @pkpoi/142970).
Hezbollah warned it will not allow further Israeli advances and remains vigilant on the front (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372859).
Violent clashes between Joulani gangs in the Ghazlaniya area of Damascus countryside resulted in house and vehicle arson and several killings (Source: @naya_foriraq/79892).
Explosions were heard and smoke plumes observed in Diyala Province, Iraq (Source: @shin_persian/7196).
An American source reported Israel may withdraw from parts of south Lebanon, with Lebanese armed forces taking control (Source: @pkpoi/142966).

sections.executive_summary: IDF operations in south Lebanon killed multiple Hezbollah fighters and struck Beit Yahoun, while Iran‑linked forces attacked shipping in the Strait of Hormuz and Gulf of Oman. Hezbollah warned it will block further Israeli advances, and an American source indicated possible Israeli withdrawal from parts of south Lebanon. Clan violence and explosions in Syria and Iraq add to the regional instability.

points: Iran‑linked Revolutionary Guard forces attacked a Singapore‑flagged cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns over maritime security (Source: @naya_foriraq/79887).
In the Gulf of Oman, Revolutionary Guard naval units fired a shell at a civilian cargo ship lacking passage permits, violating international maritime norms (Source: @pkpoi/142970).
Gulf states reportedly agreed that Iran should not collect payments in the Hormuz area, indicating regional pressure on Tehran (Source: @asafroz/50730).
A report claimed the commander of the Iranian hacker group “Handala” was killed, highlighting internal security actions within Iran (Source: @inon_yttach/9157).
The IDF’s 36th Division’s Golani combat team identified and eliminated five Hezbollah militants in Zoter A‑Sharkiya, south Lebanon (Source: @idf_telegram/24445).
An IDF combat team from the Commando Brigade neutralized a Hezbollah gunman on Mount Ali Tahr, with air force support (Source: @idf_telegram/24445).
Israeli airstrikes targeted the town of Beit Yahoun in southern Lebanon, continuing a pattern of ceasefire violations (Source: @presstv/195650).
Israel claimed to have targeted six Hezbollah members in south Lebanon, stating they were killed (Source: @SharghDaily/168091).
A reserve soldier from the 7th Armored Brigade was killed when his refueling truck overturned in the Rab al‑Thalathine area of south Lebanon (Source: @manniefabian).
Hezbollah warned it will not allow further Israeli advances and remains vigilant on the front lines (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372859).
An IDF attack on a motorcycle was reported in Zoter A‑Sharkiya, south Lebanon (Source: @inon_yttach/9164).
Israeli forces conducted artillery strikes on Beit Yahoun and nearby Kunin, as shown on live maps (Source: @redlinkleb/28257).
A senior commander warned that if Israel does not withdraw from south Lebanon today, it will be forced to flee tomorrow (Source: @inon_yttach/9163).
An American source suggested Israel may withdraw from parts of south Lebanon, with Lebanese armed forces assuming control (Source: @pkpoi/142966).
Intense clashes between Joulani gangs in the Ghazlaniya area of Damascus countryside led to house and vehicle arson and multiple killings (Source: @naya_foriraq/79892).
Explosions were heard in Diyala Province, Iraq, accompanied by smoke plumes in al‑Miqdadiyah, indicating an unknown blast (Source: @shin_persian/7196; @naya_foriraq/79878).
Local sources reported gathering of Joulani forces on the Lebanon‑Syria border, suggesting possible cross‑border militia activity (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74355).
Iranian Revolutionary Guard attacked a Singapore‑flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz, threatening commercial navigation (Source: @naya_foriraq/79887).
Revolutionary Guard naval units fired a shell at a civilian cargo vessel in the Gulf of Oman without proper clearance (Source: @pkpoi/142970).
Gulf countries agreed that Iran should not collect fees or taxes in the Hormuz maritime corridor, reflecting coordinated pressure (Source: @asafroz/50730).

key_developments: IDF combat teams eliminated five Hezbollah militants in Zoter A‑Sharkiya, south Lebanon, with air support (Source: @idf_telegram/24445).
Israeli airstrikes hit Beit Yahoun in southern Lebanon, continuing violations of the ceasefire (Source: @presstv/195650).
Iranian Revolutionary Guard forces attacked a Singapore‑flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz, raising maritime security concerns (Source: @naya_foriraq/79887).
Revolutionary Guard naval units shelled a civilian cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman without passage permits (Source: @pkpoi/142970).
Hezbollah issued a warning that it will prevent any further Israeli advances on the front line (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/372859).
Violent Joulani clan clashes in the Ghazlaniya area of Damascus countryside resulted in arson and multiple deaths (Source: @naya_foriraq/79892).
Explosions and smoke plumes were reported in Diyala Province, Iraq, indicating an unknown explosive event (Source: @shin_persian/7196).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah remains a potent threat on the Israel‑Lebanon frontier, actively engaging IDF forces and warning against further incursions. Iran's Revolutionary Guard is extending its asymmetric warfare to maritime domains, targeting commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz and Gulf of Oman, which could disrupt regional trade. Clan violence in Syria and unexplained explosions in Iraq suggest deteriorating internal security that may spill over borders, complicating the operational environment for all actors.
sections.regional_response: Lebanese armed forces may assume control of areas vacated by Israeli troops, as indicated by an American source, potentially altering the balance of power in south Lebanon. Gulf states are coordinating to limit Iran's revenue streams from maritime corridors, reflecting a unified stance against Tehran's coercive tactics. International maritime agencies are likely to increase monitoring of the Hormuz and Oman waterways following recent attacks.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah's deployment patterns in south Lebanon is critical, especially around Zoter A‑Sharkiya and Mount Ali Tahr. Surveillance of Iranian naval activity in the Hormuz Strait and Gulf of Oman should be intensified to anticipate further attacks on commercial vessels. Intelligence collection on Joulani militia movements along the Syria‑Lebanon border is needed to assess the risk of cross‑border escalation.
